name: Bleak (Bluetooth Low Energy for Python)

auth.notes: BLE pairing/bonding handled by OS; Bleak uses OS BLE stack

performance.notes: BLE latency inherent to protocol; Python async wraps OS BLE

agent_readiness.notes: Async Python BLE (Bluetooth Low Energy) client library. from bleak import BleakScanner, BleakClient. devices = await BleakScanner.discover(). for d in devices: print(d.name, d.address). async with BleakClient('AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF') as client: services = await client.get_services(); for service in services: print(service). value = await client.read_gatt_char(characteristic_uuid). await client.write_gatt_char(uuid, bytearray([0x01, 0x02])). Notifications: await client.start_notify(uuid, callback). def callback(sender, data): print(data). client.is_connected. Pairing: await client.pair(). Works on macOS (CoreBluetooth), Windows (WinRT), Linux (BlueZ). Scan with filter: BleakScanner.discover(service_uuids=['180D']). GATT: Generic Attribute Profile.

gotchas: Bluetooth permissions required — macOS needs NSBluetoothAlwaysUsageDescription
Linux requires BlueZ and may need sudo/dbus permissions
BLE device addressing differs by OS (UUID on macOS vs MAC on Linux)
Connections can drop — implement reconnection logic
Threading issues if mixing sync and async code
best_when: Communicating with BLE sensors, wearables, IoT devices from Python
Reading fitness tracker or heart rate monitor data
Connecting to BLE hardware on macOS, Windows, or Linux
avoid_when: When direct platform APIs are preferred over abstraction
summary: Bleak is an async Python BLE (Bluetooth Low Energy) client. BleakScanner.discover(); async with BleakClient('MAC') as client: read_gatt_char(uuid); write_gatt_char(uuid, data); start_notify(uuid, callback). Cross-platform: macOS CoreBluetooth, Windows WinRT, Linux BlueZ. MIT by hbldh. Bluetooth permissions required. Device addresses differ by OS. Best for Python BLE sensor/wearable communication.
